text:
(Specify any additional respondents)

(Specify if any adult party acts by a litigation friend)

(Specify if the children or any of them act by a children’s guardian)

Recitals

(amend as appropriate) This order was made at a hearing at which both parties were present and represented.

The judge read the following affidavits/witness statements [insert] and heard oral evidence from [name(s)].

The judge refused the application on the basis that the applicant had not established that [the [removal] / [retention] [was in breach of the rights of custody of the applicant] / [the child was habitually resident in [insert] immediately prior to the [removal] / [retention]].

The court records that, as: (a) the courts of [requesting State] have under consideration proceedings falling within the scope of the 1996 Hague Convention; and (b) which were commenced at a time that court had substantive jurisdiction pursuant to Articles 5-10, Article 13(1) of that Convention is engaged.

The court records that the courts of [requesting State] [have] / [have not] been seized of an application relating to matters of parental responsibility concerning the child[ren].

The orders in respect of the child[ren] are made pursuant to [Article 20 BIIR] / [Article 13 BIIR] / [Article 8 BIIR] / [Article 11 1996 Hague Convention] / [Article 12 1996 Hague Convention] / [Article 5 1996 Hague Convention].

[Insert any further recitals]

Undertakings to the court by the applicant [applicant name] or respondent [respondent name]

[Insert any undertakings (include an undertakings form in addition)]

IT IS ORDERED THAT:

The application for summary return pursuant to the 1980 Hague Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction is dismissed.

[A transcript of the judgment given today shall be prepared [at public expense] / [at the parties’ expense] [on an expedited basis] and provided to the ICACU.]

[The ICACU shall immediately transmit the judgment and this order to the [insert] Central Authority.]

Permission is granted for the parties to disclose any document filed in these proceedings and the judgment in this matter to [their legal advisors] / [the [name of court] court].

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 2: Children and Other Orders (May 2026)
metadata.title: In the High Court of Justice
